title: Successful password reset via valid email
preconditions: User has a registered account with a verified email address. User is on the login page.
steps: Navigate to the TaskFlow login page,Click on 'Forgot Password' link,Enter a valid registered email address in the input field,Click the 'Send Reset Link' button,Open the email inbox and locate the password reset email,Click the reset link in the email,Enter a new valid password and confirm it,Click 'Reset Password' button
expected_result: Password is successfully reset. User receives a confirmation message and is able to log in with the new password.

title: Password reset attempt with unregistered email
preconditions: User is on the 'Forgot Password' page. The email address to be used does not exist in the system.
steps: Navigate to the TaskFlow login page,Click on 'Forgot Password' link,Enter an email address that is NOT registered in the system,Click the 'Send Reset Link' button
expected_result: System displays a generic message such as 'If this email is registered, you will receive a reset link.' No reset email is sent. No account or user data is exposed.

title: Password reset with malformed or empty email input
preconditions: User is on the 'Forgot Password' page.
steps: Navigate to the TaskFlow 'Forgot Password' page,Leave the email field empty and click 'Send Reset Link',Re-attempt by entering a malformed email (e.g., 'user@', 'notanemail', '@domain.com'),Click 'Send Reset Link' for each malformed input
expected_result: System displays an appropriate validation error message (e.g., 'Please enter a valid email address') and does not submit the form or send any email.

title: Password reset link expiry boundary check
preconditions: User has requested a password reset and received a reset link via email. The system has a defined expiry time for reset links (e.g., 60 minutes).
steps: Request a password reset for a valid registered email,Wait until the reset link is just about to expire (e.g., at 59 minutes 50 seconds),Click the reset link and attempt to reset the password,Complete the password reset flow,Request another reset link and wait until it has fully expired (e.g., 61 minutes),Click the expired reset link and attempt to reset the password
expected_result: A link used just before expiry (within valid window) allows password reset successfully. A link used after expiry displays an error message such as 'This reset link has expired. Please request a new one.' and does not allow password reset.

title: Password reset link cannot be reused after successful use
preconditions: User has received a valid password reset link via email and has already used it to successfully reset their password.
steps: Request a password reset for a valid registered email,Click the reset link from the email,Enter and confirm a new valid password,Click 'Reset Password' and confirm the reset is successful,Copy and re-open the same reset link URL in the browser,Attempt to reset the password again using the same link
expected_result: The system rejects the reused link and displays an error such as 'This reset link has already been used or is invalid.' User cannot reset the password again using the old link.

title: Successful password reset via valid registered email
preconditions: User has a registered account with a valid email address. The email service is operational. User is on the login page.
steps: Navigate to the login page,Click on 'Forgot Password' link,Enter a valid registered email address in the password reset form,Click the 'Send Reset Link' button,Open the email inbox and locate the password reset email,Click the password reset link in the email,Enter a new valid password and confirm it,Click 'Reset Password' button,Attempt to log in with the new password
expected_result: User receives a password reset email within a reasonable time (e.g., 2 minutes), is able to set a new password, and can successfully log in with the updated credentials. The old password no longer works.

title: Password reset attempt with unregistered email address
preconditions: User is on the 'Forgot Password' page. The email address to be used is not registered in the system.
steps: Navigate to the 'Forgot Password' page,Enter an email address that is not associated with any account (e.g., notregistered@example.com),Click the 'Send Reset Link' button,Check the inbox of the unregistered email for any received messages
expected_result: The system displays a generic confirmation message (e.g., 'If this email is registered, you will receive a reset link') to prevent user enumeration. No password reset email is sent to the unregistered address.

title: Password reset link expiry boundary - link used after expiration
preconditions: User has requested a password reset and received the reset link via email. The system enforces a reset link expiration time (e.g., 1 hour).
steps: Request a password reset for a valid registered email,Wait until the reset link has expired (e.g., after 1 hour or as per system configuration),Click the expired password reset link from the email,Attempt to enter and submit a new password using the expired link
expected_result: The system displays an error message indicating the reset link has expired or is no longer valid. The user is prompted to request a new password reset link. The password is not changed.

title: Password reset form submitted with empty email field
preconditions: User is on the 'Forgot Password' page.
steps: Navigate to the 'Forgot Password' page,Leave the email input field blank,Click the 'Send Reset Link' button
expected_result: The system displays a validation error message indicating the email field is required (e.g., 'Email address is required'). No request is sent to the server and no reset email is dispatched.

title: Password reset link can only be used once (single-use token)
preconditions: User has received a valid, unexpired password reset link via email and has already used it to successfully reset the password.
steps: Request a password reset for a valid registered email,Click the reset link and successfully set a new password,Copy and re-use the same reset link URL in a new browser session,Attempt to set another new password using the already-used link
expected_result: The system rejects the reused reset link and displays an error message (e.g., 'This reset link has already been used or is invalid'). The password is not changed again, protecting the account from replay attacks.

title: Successful password reset via valid email
preconditions: User has a registered account with a valid email address. User is on the login page.
steps: Navigate to the login page,Click on 'Forgot Password' link,Enter a valid registered email address,Click 'Send Reset Link' button,Open the email inbox and locate the password reset email,Click the password reset link in the email,Enter a new valid password and confirm it,Click 'Reset Password' button
expected_result: Password is successfully reset. User receives a confirmation message and is able to log in with the new password.

title: Password reset attempt with unregistered email
preconditions: User is on the 'Forgot Password' page.
steps: Navigate to the 'Forgot Password' page,Enter an email address that is not registered in the system,Click 'Send Reset Link' button
expected_result: System displays an appropriate error message such as 'Email address not found' and does not send a reset email.

title: Password reset with an invalid email format
preconditions: User is on the 'Forgot Password' page.
steps: Navigate to the 'Forgot Password' page,Enter a malformed email address (e.g., 'user@' or 'notanemail'),Click 'Send Reset Link' button
expected_result: System displays a validation error indicating the email format is invalid and does not proceed with sending a reset link.

title: Password reset link expiry boundary check
preconditions: User has received a valid password reset email. The reset link has a defined expiry time (e.g., 60 minutes).
steps: Navigate to the 'Forgot Password' page,Enter a valid registered email address and click 'Send Reset Link',Wait until the reset link is exactly at its expiry threshold (e.g., 60 minutes),Click the password reset link from the email,Attempt to enter and submit a new password
expected_result: System rejects the expired reset link and displays a message such as 'This link has expired. Please request a new password reset.'

title: Password reset link can only be used once
preconditions: User has successfully reset their password using a reset link received via email.
steps: Complete a successful password reset using the emailed link,Attempt to click the same password reset link again,Observe the system response
expected_result: System rejects the reuse of the already-used reset link and displays an appropriate message such as 'This reset link is no longer valid.'

title: Successful password reset via valid email
preconditions: User has a registered and verified account with a valid email address. User is on the login page.
steps: Navigate to the login page,Click on 'Forgot Password' link,Enter a valid registered email address,Click 'Send Reset Link' button,Open the email inbox and locate the password reset email,Click on the password reset link in the email,Enter a new valid password and confirm it,Click 'Reset Password' button,Attempt to log in with the new password
expected_result: User receives a password reset email, is able to set a new password successfully, and can log in with the new credentials. Old password no longer works.

title: Password reset request with unregistered email
preconditions: User is on the 'Forgot Password' page. The email address to be used does not exist in the system.
steps: Navigate to the 'Forgot Password' page,Enter an email address that is not registered in the system,Click 'Send Reset Link' button,Check the inbox of the unregistered email address for any reset email
expected_result: The system displays a generic confirmation message (e.g., 'If this email is registered, a reset link will be sent') to prevent user enumeration. No password reset email is sent to the unregistered address.

title: Password reset link expiry boundary check
preconditions: User has requested a password reset and received the reset email. The system has a defined token expiry period (e.g., 60 minutes).
steps: Request a password reset for a registered email,Wait until the reset link is exactly at expiry time (e.g., 60 minutes),Click the password reset link immediately at the boundary,Attempt to click the same link 1 minute after the expiry period,Enter a new password and submit on the expired link
expected_result: The link is valid exactly at the expiry boundary (60 minutes) and allows password reset. After the expiry period (61 minutes), the link is rejected with an appropriate error message such as 'This link has expired. Please request a new one.'

title: Password reset link cannot be reused after successful reset
preconditions: User has successfully reset their password using a valid reset link.
steps: Complete a successful password reset using the emailed link,Copy and store the used password reset link URL,Attempt to open the used reset link again in the browser,Try to submit a new password using the already-used link
expected_result: The system rejects the already-used reset link and displays an error message such as 'This reset link has already been used or is invalid.' No password change is made.

title: Password reset with invalid email format
preconditions: User is on the 'Forgot Password' page.
steps: Navigate to the 'Forgot Password' page,Enter an invalid email format (e.g., 'userexample.com', 'user@', '@example.com', 'plaintext'),Click 'Send Reset Link' button for each invalid format
expected_result: The system displays an inline validation error message (e.g., 'Please enter a valid email address') for each invalid email format and does not submit the request or send any email.

title: Successful user registration with valid email and password
preconditions: The user is not already registered in the system. The registration page is accessible.
steps: Navigate to the registration page,Enter a valid email address (e.g., newuser@example.com),Enter a valid password that meets system requirements (e.g., Password@123),Confirm the password in the confirm password field,Click the 'Register' button
expected_result: The user is successfully registered, a confirmation message is displayed, and a verification/welcome email is sent to the provided email address.

title: Registration attempt with an already registered email address
preconditions: An account with the email address 'existing@example.com' already exists in the system.
steps: Navigate to the registration page,Enter the already registered email address (e.g., existing@example.com),Enter a valid password (e.g., Password@123),Confirm the password,Click the 'Register' button
expected_result: The system displays an appropriate error message indicating that the email address is already in use and registration is not completed.

title: Registration attempt with an invalid email format
preconditions: The registration page is accessible.
steps: Navigate to the registration page,Enter an invalid email format (e.g., 'notanemail', 'user@', '@domain.com'),Enter a valid password (e.g., Password@123),Confirm the password,Click the 'Register' button
expected_result: The system displays a validation error indicating that the email address format is invalid and does not proceed with registration.

title: Registration with a password at the minimum allowed length boundary
preconditions: The registration page is accessible and the system requires a minimum password length (e.g., 8 characters).
steps: Navigate to the registration page,Enter a valid new email address (e.g., boundaryuser@example.com),Enter a password exactly at the minimum allowed length (e.g., 8 characters: 'Pass@123'),Confirm the password,Click the 'Register' button
expected_result: The user is successfully registered with the minimum-length password, and a confirmation message or email is sent.

title: Registration attempt with an empty email and/or password field
preconditions: The registration page is accessible.
steps: Navigate to the registration page,Leave the email address field empty,Leave the password field empty,Click the 'Register' button
expected_result: The system displays validation error messages for both the email and password fields indicating they are required, and registration is not completed.

title: Valid password meeting all requirements
preconditions: User is on the password creation or reset page
steps: Navigate to the password creation or reset form,Enter a password that is at least 8 characters long, contains at least one number and one special character (e.g., 'Secure1!'),Submit the form
expected_result: Password is accepted and the user account is created or updated successfully

title: Password shorter than 8 characters is rejected
preconditions: User is on the password creation or reset page
steps: Navigate to the password creation or reset form,Enter a password that is fewer than 8 characters (e.g., 'Ab1!'),Submit the form
expected_result: System displays an error message indicating the password must be at least 8 characters long and does not save the password

title: Password missing a number is rejected
preconditions: User is on the password creation or reset page
steps: Navigate to the password creation or reset form,Enter a password that is at least 8 characters and contains a special character but no number (e.g., 'Password!'),Submit the form
expected_result: System displays an error message indicating the password must contain at least one number and does not save the password

title: Password missing a special character is rejected
preconditions: User is on the password creation or reset page
steps: Navigate to the password creation or reset form,Enter a password that is at least 8 characters and contains a number but no special character (e.g., 'Password1'),Submit the form
expected_result: System displays an error message indicating the password must contain at least one special character and does not save the password

title: Password of exactly 8 characters with required complexity is accepted
preconditions: User is on the password creation or reset page
steps: Navigate to the password creation or reset form,Enter a password that is exactly 8 characters long and contains at least one number and one special character (e.g., 'Pass1!ab'),Submit the form
expected_result: Password is accepted as valid and the account is created or updated successfully, confirming the boundary value of 8 characters is inclusive

title: Verify that a verification email with a unique link is sent upon password reset request
preconditions: User has a registered account with a valid email address. User has initiated a password reset request.
steps: Navigate to the password reset page,Enter a valid registered email address,Click the 'Send Reset Email' button,Open the email inbox for the registered email address,Check for the received verification email
expected_result: A verification email is received containing a unique reset link. The link is functional and redirects the user to the password reset page.

title: Verify that the verification link expires after 24 hours
preconditions: User has a registered account with a valid email address. A password reset email has been sent and the verification link has been received. System clock or test environment supports time simulation.
steps: Trigger a password reset request for a registered user,Receive the verification email and copy the unique link,Advance the system time or wait until 24 hours and 1 minute have elapsed since the email was issued,Open the copied verification link in a browser,Observe the system response
expected_result: The system displays an error message indicating the link has expired and does not allow the user to reset their password using this link.

title: Verify that the verification link is still valid just before the 24-hour expiry
preconditions: User has a registered account with a valid email address. A password reset email has been sent. System clock or test environment supports time simulation.
steps: Trigger a password reset request for a registered user,Receive the verification email and copy the unique link,Advance the system time to 23 hours and 59 minutes after the email was issued,Open the copied verification link in a browser,Attempt to reset the password using the link
expected_result: The verification link is still valid and the user is able to successfully access the password reset page and change their password.

title: Verify that each password reset request generates a unique verification link
preconditions: User has a registered account with a valid email address.
steps: Navigate to the password reset page,Enter a valid registered email address and submit the reset request,Record the unique link received in the first verification email,Submit a second password reset request for the same email address,Record the unique link received in the second verification email,Compare the two links
expected_result: Both verification links are different (unique). The first link is invalidated upon issuance of the second link or independently unique per request.

title: Verify that an already-used verification link cannot be reused
preconditions: User has a registered account with a valid email address. A password reset email has been sent and the link has not yet expired.
steps: Trigger a password reset request and receive the verification email,Open the verification link and successfully reset the password,Attempt to open the same verification link again in a browser
expected_result: The system displays an error message indicating the link is invalid or has already been used, and does not allow another password reset via this link.

title: Successful login with valid email and password
preconditions: User is registered in the system with a verified email address and a known password. The user is on the login page.
steps: Navigate to the login page,Enter a valid registered email address in the email field,Enter the correct password in the password field,Click the 'Login' button
expected_result: User is successfully authenticated and redirected to the application dashboard or home page.

title: Login attempt with invalid password
preconditions: User is registered in the system. The user is on the login page.
steps: Navigate to the login page,Enter a valid registered email address in the email field,Enter an incorrect password in the password field,Click the 'Login' button
expected_result: Login is denied. An appropriate error message is displayed (e.g., 'Invalid email or password'). The user remains on the login page.

title: Login attempt with unregistered email address
preconditions: The user is on the login page. The email address to be used does not exist in the system.
steps: Navigate to the login page,Enter an email address that is not registered in the system,Enter any password in the password field,Click the 'Login' button
expected_result: Login is denied. A generic error message is displayed (e.g., 'Invalid email or password') without revealing whether the email exists. The user remains on the login page.

title: Login attempt with empty email and password fields
preconditions: The user is on the login page.
steps: Navigate to the login page,Leave the email field empty,Leave the password field empty,Click the 'Login' button
expected_result: Login is not submitted. Validation error messages are displayed for both the email and password fields indicating they are required. The user remains on the login page.

title: Login attempt using SQL injection in email field
preconditions: The user is on the login page.
steps: Navigate to the login page,Enter a SQL injection string in the email field (e.g., "' OR '1'='1'; --"),Enter any value in the password field,Click the 'Login' button
expected_result: Login is denied. The system sanitizes the input and does not execute any unintended SQL commands. An appropriate error message is displayed and no unauthorized access is granted.

title: Account locks after exactly 5 consecutive failed login attempts
preconditions: User account exists and is currently unlocked. User has a valid username.
steps: Navigate to the login page,Enter valid username and an incorrect password,Click the 'Login' button,Repeat steps 2-3 four more times (total of 5 failed attempts),Observe the system response after the 5th failed attempt,Attempt to log in with the correct password immediately after
expected_result: After the 5th consecutive failed login attempt, the account is locked. A message is displayed informing the user that the account has been locked for 15 minutes. Login with correct credentials is denied during the lockout period.

title: Account remains accessible after 4 consecutive failed login attempts
preconditions: User account exists and is currently unlocked. User has a valid username and password.
steps: Navigate to the login page,Enter valid username and an incorrect password,Click the 'Login' button,Repeat steps 2-3 three more times (total of 4 failed attempts),Enter the valid username and correct password,Click the 'Login' button
expected_result: After 4 consecutive failed attempts the account is NOT locked. On the 5th attempt with correct credentials, the user is successfully logged in and the failed attempt counter is reset.

title: Account unlocks automatically after 15-minute lockout period
preconditions: User account has been locked due to 5 consecutive failed login attempts. Lockout start time is recorded.
steps: Confirm the account is locked by attempting to log in with correct credentials immediately after lockout,Wait exactly 15 minutes from the time the account was locked,Navigate to the login page,Enter the valid username and correct password,Click the 'Login' button
expected_result: After exactly 15 minutes have elapsed, the account is automatically unlocked. The user can successfully log in with valid credentials. No manual intervention is required.

title: Login attempt with correct credentials before 15-minute lockout expires is rejected
preconditions: User account has just been locked due to 5 consecutive failed login attempts.
steps: Wait 14 minutes and 50 seconds after the account lockout begins,Navigate to the login page,Enter valid username and correct password,Click the 'Login' button,Observe the system response
expected_result: The login attempt is denied. A message informs the user that the account is still locked and indicates the remaining lockout duration. The account remains locked until the full 15 minutes have passed.

title: Failed login attempt counter resets after a successful login
preconditions: User account exists and is unlocked. User has valid credentials.
steps: Navigate to the login page,Enter valid username and an incorrect password and click 'Login' — repeat 3 times,Enter the valid username and correct password and click 'Login' to successfully log in,Log out of the account,Enter valid username and an incorrect password and click 'Login' — repeat 3 more times,Verify the account is not locked after this second set of failed attempts,Enter valid username and an incorrect password 2 more times to reach 5 total in the new session,Observe the system response on the 5th failed attempt in the new session
expected_result: Successful login in step 3 resets the failed attempt counter to zero. A fresh sequence of 5 consecutive failed attempts is required to trigger a new lockout. The account is locked only after 5 consecutive failures in the second sequence.

title: Successful password reset via valid registered email
preconditions: User has a registered and verified email account in the system. User is on the login page.
steps: Navigate to the login page,Click on 'Forgot Password' link,Enter a valid registered email address,Click 'Send Reset Link' button,Open the registered email inbox,Click the password reset link received in the email,Enter a new valid password,Confirm the new password,Click 'Reset Password' button,Attempt to log in with the new password
expected_result: User receives a password reset email, successfully resets the password, and can log in with the new password. Old password no longer works.

title: Password reset attempt with unregistered email address
preconditions: User is on the 'Forgot Password' page. The email address to be used does not exist in the system.
steps: Navigate to the 'Forgot Password' page,Enter an email address that is not registered in the system,Click 'Send Reset Link' button,Check the entered email inbox for any reset email
expected_result: System displays a generic message such as 'If this email is registered, you will receive a reset link' without revealing whether the email exists. No reset email is sent to the unregistered address.

title: Password reset link expiry boundary check
preconditions: User has requested a password reset and received a reset link via email. The system has a defined token expiry time (e.g., 60 minutes).
steps: Request a password reset for a valid registered email,Wait until the reset link is just at the boundary of expiry (e.g., exactly 60 minutes),Click the password reset link in the email,Attempt to enter and submit a new password
expected_result: If accessed at exactly the expiry boundary, the system should reject the token and display an 'expired link' or 'invalid token' error message, prompting the user to request a new reset link.

title: Password reset with invalid email format
preconditions: User is on the 'Forgot Password' page.
steps: Navigate to the 'Forgot Password' page,Enter an invalidly formatted email address (e.g., 'user@', 'userexample.com', 'user @example.com'),Click 'Send Reset Link' button
expected_result: System displays a validation error indicating the email format is invalid and does not attempt to send a reset email.

title: Password reset link cannot be reused after successful reset
preconditions: User has already successfully reset their password using a valid reset link received via email.
steps: Complete a successful password reset using the emailed reset link,Re-open or copy and re-navigate to the same password reset link,Attempt to submit a new password using the already-used link
expected_result: System rejects the previously used reset link and displays an error such as 'This reset link has already been used or is invalid.' User is prompted to request a new reset link.

title: Successfully invite a new team member by email
preconditions: User is logged in as an organization administrator. The organization has an active account. The email address to be invited is not already associated with the organization.
steps: Log in as an organization administrator.,Navigate to the 'Team Members' or 'Invite Members' section.,Click on the 'Invite Member' button.,Enter a valid email address (e.g., newmember@example.com) in the invitation field.,Click the 'Send Invitation' button.
expected_result: The system sends an invitation email to the specified address. A success confirmation message is displayed. The invited email appears in the 'Pending Invitations' list.

title: Non-admin user cannot invite team members
preconditions: User is logged in as a regular (non-admin) team member of the organization.
steps: Log in as a regular (non-admin) organization member.,Attempt to navigate to the 'Team Members' or 'Invite Members' section.,Attempt to access the 'Invite Member' functionality directly via URL if the UI option is not visible.
expected_result: The invite member option is not visible or accessible to non-admin users. Any direct URL access returns an authorization error (e.g., 403 Forbidden). No invitation is sent.

title: Invite team member with an invalid email format
preconditions: User is logged in as an organization administrator.
steps: Log in as an organization administrator.,Navigate to the 'Invite Members' section.,Click on the 'Invite Member' button.,Enter an invalid email address (e.g., 'notanemail', 'user@', '@domain.com').,Click the 'Send Invitation' button.
expected_result: The system displays a validation error message indicating that the email address format is invalid. No invitation email is sent.

title: Invite a team member who is already part of the organization
preconditions: User is logged in as an organization administrator. The email address to be invited is already an active member of the organization.
steps: Log in as an organization administrator.,Navigate to the 'Invite Members' section.,Click on the 'Invite Member' button.,Enter the email address of an existing active member of the organization.,Click the 'Send Invitation' button.
expected_result: The system displays an error or warning message indicating that the user is already a member of the organization. No duplicate invitation or membership is created.

title: Invite team member with an empty email field
preconditions: User is logged in as an organization administrator.
steps: Log in as an organization administrator.,Navigate to the 'Invite Members' section.,Click on the 'Invite Member' button.,Leave the email address field completely empty.,Click the 'Send Invitation' button.
expected_result: The system displays a validation error message indicating that the email field is required and cannot be empty. No invitation is sent.

title: Verify Administrator role has full system permissions
preconditions: A user account with Administrator role exists and is active in the system.
steps: Log in with Administrator credentials.,Navigate to user management section.,Attempt to create a new user.,Attempt to delete an existing user.,Attempt to modify system settings.,Attempt to view all projects and contributors.
expected_result: Administrator can successfully perform all actions: create/delete users, modify system settings, and view all projects and contributors without any permission errors.

title: Verify Project Manager role has project-scoped permissions only
preconditions: A user account with Project Manager role exists and is active. At least one project is assigned to this Project Manager.
steps: Log in with Project Manager credentials.,Navigate to the assigned project and attempt to create/edit tasks.,Attempt to add or remove Contributors from the assigned project.,Attempt to access system administration settings.,Attempt to manage another Project Manager's project.
expected_result: Project Manager can create/edit tasks and manage Contributors within their own project. Accessing system administration settings and managing other Project Managers' projects should be denied with an appropriate 'Access Denied' message.

title: Verify Contributor role is restricted to read and contribution actions only
preconditions: A user account with Contributor role exists and is assigned to at least one project.
steps: Log in with Contributor credentials.,Navigate to the assigned project and attempt to view tasks.,Attempt to update the status of an assigned task.,Attempt to delete a task.,Attempt to access user management or system settings.,Attempt to create a new project.
expected_result: Contributor can view tasks and update status of assigned tasks. Deleting tasks, accessing user management, system settings, and creating new projects should be denied with an appropriate 'Access Denied' message.

title: Verify that a Contributor cannot escalate their own role to Administrator
preconditions: A user account with Contributor role exists. The system API or UI for role management is accessible.
steps: Log in with Contributor credentials.,Attempt to access the role management API endpoint directly (e.g., via HTTP request) to change own role to 'Administrator'.,Attempt to navigate to any admin UI page to modify own profile role.,Verify the role remains 'Contributor' after all attempts.
expected_result: All attempts to escalate the role are rejected with a 403 Forbidden or equivalent error. The user's role remains 'Contributor' in the system.

title: Verify system behavior when a user has no assigned role
preconditions: A user account exists in the system with no role assigned (role field is null or empty).
steps: Attempt to log in with the role-less user account.,If login is permitted, attempt to access project list.,Attempt to access user management.,Attempt to view system settings.
expected_result: The system either prevents login for a role-less user with a clear error message, or if login is permitted, all protected resources return 'Access Denied'. The system does not default to any elevated permissions.

title: Administrator successfully deactivates an active user account
preconditions: Administrator is logged in with valid admin credentials. A target user account exists and is currently active.
steps: Log in as Administrator.,Navigate to the User Management section.,Search for and select an active user account.,Click the 'Deactivate Account' button.,Confirm the deactivation action in the confirmation dialog.,Verify the user account status is updated to 'Inactive'.
expected_result: The user account is marked as 'Inactive' and a success confirmation message is displayed to the Administrator.

title: Deactivated user's access is revoked immediately upon deactivation
preconditions: Administrator is logged in. The target user is currently logged in with an active session. The target user account is active.
steps: Log in as Administrator in one browser session.,Ensure the target user is logged in and has an active session in a separate browser session.,As Administrator, navigate to User Management and locate the target user.,Deactivate the target user's account and confirm the action.,Immediately switch to the target user's session and attempt to perform any action (e.g., navigate to a protected page or make an API request).,Observe the response received by the target user.
expected_result: The target user's session is immediately invalidated. The user is either redirected to the login page or receives an 'Access Denied / Account Deactivated' message without any delay.

title: Non-administrator user attempts to deactivate a user account
preconditions: A non-administrator user (e.g., regular user or moderator) is logged in. At least one other active user account exists in the system.
steps: Log in as a non-administrator user.,Attempt to navigate directly to the User Management section via URL or menu.,Attempt to call the deactivate user API endpoint directly with a valid user ID.,Observe the system response.
expected_result: The system denies access and returns a '403 Forbidden' or 'Unauthorized Access' error. The target user account remains active and unaffected.

title: Administrator attempts to deactivate an already deactivated user account
preconditions: Administrator is logged in. A user account already in 'Inactive' status exists in the system.
steps: Log in as Administrator.,Navigate to the User Management section.,Search for and select a user account that is already in 'Inactive' status.,Attempt to click the 'Deactivate Account' button or trigger the deactivation action.,Observe the system response.
expected_result: The system displays an appropriate informational message such as 'Account is already deactivated' and does not perform any duplicate deactivation action. No errors or system crashes occur.

title: Administrator attempts to deactivate a non-existent user account via API
preconditions: Administrator is authenticated and has a valid session token. A user ID that does not exist in the system is known.
steps: Log in as Administrator and obtain a valid session token.,Construct a deactivation API request using a non-existent or invalid user ID (e.g., ID: 999999 or a randomly generated UUID).,Send the API request to the deactivate user endpoint.,Observe the HTTP status code and response body.
expected_result: The system returns a '404 Not Found' error with an appropriate error message such as 'User not found'. No unintended side effects or system errors occur.

title: Successfully update all profile fields (display name, avatar, time zone)
preconditions: User is logged in with a valid account. Profile page is accessible.
steps: Navigate to the user profile settings page.,Enter a new valid display name (e.g., 'John Doe').,Upload a valid avatar image (e.g., JPEG, under 2MB).,Select a valid time zone from the dropdown (e.g., 'UTC+05:30 Asia/Kolkata').,Click the 'Save' or 'Update Profile' button.,Verify the profile page reflects the updated information.
expected_result: All three fields (display name, avatar image, and time zone) are saved successfully. A success confirmation message is displayed and the profile page shows the updated values.

title: Update only the display name leaving other fields unchanged
preconditions: User is logged in. Profile already has an existing avatar and time zone set.
steps: Navigate to the user profile settings page.,Clear the current display name field and enter a new display name (e.g., 'Jane Smith').,Leave the avatar image and time zone fields unchanged.,Click the 'Save' or 'Update Profile' button.,Verify the profile page reflects the updated display name.,Verify the avatar and time zone remain unchanged.
expected_result: Only the display name is updated. Avatar and time zone retain their previous values. A success confirmation message is displayed.

title: Attempt to update display name with an empty string
preconditions: User is logged in. Profile page is accessible with an existing display name.
steps: Navigate to the user profile settings page.,Clear the display name field completely leaving it blank.,Click the 'Save' or 'Update Profile' button.,Observe the system response.
expected_result: The system displays a validation error message indicating that the display name cannot be empty. The profile is not updated and the previous display name is preserved.

title: Attempt to upload an invalid avatar image format
preconditions: User is logged in. Profile page is accessible.
steps: Navigate to the user profile settings page.,Attempt to upload a file with an unsupported format (e.g., a .exe or .pdf file) as the avatar image.,Click the 'Save' or 'Update Profile' button.,Observe the system response.
expected_result: The system rejects the file and displays an appropriate error message indicating that only supported image formats (e.g., JPEG, PNG) are allowed. The profile avatar is not updated.

title: Update display name with maximum allowed character length
preconditions: User is logged in. The system has a defined maximum character limit for display name (assumed 50 characters).
steps: Navigate to the user profile settings page.,Enter a display name exactly at the maximum allowed character limit (e.g., a 50-character string).,Click the 'Save' or 'Update Profile' button.,Observe the system response.,Then repeat the test entering a display name that exceeds the maximum limit by one character (51 characters).,Click the 'Save' or 'Update Profile' button again.,Observe the system response.
expected_result: A display name at the exact maximum limit (50 characters) is accepted and saved successfully. A display name exceeding the limit (51 characters) is rejected with a validation error message indicating the character limit has been exceeded.

title: Administrator successfully creates a new user
preconditions: Administrator is logged into the system with valid admin credentials. The user management module is accessible.
steps: Navigate to the User Management section from the admin dashboard.,Click on 'Add New User' button.,Fill in all required fields: First Name, Last Name, Email, Role.,Click 'Save' or 'Create User' button.,Verify the new user appears in the user list.
expected_result: The new user is successfully created and appears in the user list. A confirmation message is displayed. The process completes within 3 clicks from the dashboard.

title: Administrator successfully resets a user's password via email
preconditions: Administrator is logged in. A target user account exists in the system with a valid email address.
steps: Navigate to the User Management section.,Locate the target user via search or user list.,Click on the user's profile or options menu.,Select 'Reset Password' option.,Confirm the password reset action.,Verify the system sends a reset email to the user's registered email address.
expected_result: The system triggers a password reset email to the user's registered email. A success confirmation is shown to the administrator. The user receives an email with a valid reset link.

title: Administrator attempts to create a user with an already registered email
preconditions: Administrator is logged in. A user with the email 'existing@example.com' already exists in the system.
steps: Navigate to the User Management section.,Click on 'Add New User'.,Enter 'existing@example.com' in the email field.,Fill in all other required fields.,Click 'Save' or 'Create User'.
expected_result: The system displays a clear, intuitive error message indicating the email is already in use. No duplicate user is created. The form remains populated so the administrator can correct the entry without re-entering all data.

title: Administrator attempts to create a user with missing required fields
preconditions: Administrator is logged in and on the 'Add New User' form.
steps: Navigate to the User Management section.,Click on 'Add New User'.,Leave one or more required fields (e.g., Email, Role) blank.,Click 'Save' or 'Create User'.
expected_result: The system prevents user creation and highlights the missing required fields with descriptive inline validation messages. No incomplete user record is saved.

title: Administrator manages maximum number of users in the system
preconditions: Administrator is logged in. The system is populated with the maximum allowed number of user accounts (e.g., at the defined system limit).
steps: Navigate to the User Management section.,Observe the user list rendering performance and pagination behavior.,Attempt to search for a specific user by name or email.,Attempt to add one more user beyond the maximum limit.,Verify the system response.
expected_result: The user list loads within an acceptable time and is navigable via pagination or search. If a hard limit exists, the system displays a clear, informative message when the limit is reached and prevents further user creation. If no hard limit, the new user is created successfully.

title: Create a new project successfully
preconditions: User is logged in with project management permissions. No project with the same name exists.
steps: Navigate to the Project Management section.,Click on 'Create New Project' button.,Enter a valid project name, description, start date, and end date.,Assign at least one team member.,Click 'Save' or 'Create Project'.
expected_result: Project is created successfully and appears in the project list with all provided details correctly saved.

title: Edit an existing project's details
preconditions: User is logged in with project management permissions. At least one project already exists in the system.
steps: Navigate to the Project Management section.,Select an existing project from the list.,Click the 'Edit' button.,Modify the project name and description with valid values.,Click 'Save Changes'.
expected_result: Project details are updated successfully, and the updated information is reflected in the project list and detail view.

title: Attempt to create a project with missing required fields
preconditions: User is logged in with project management permissions.
steps: Navigate to the Project Management section.,Click on 'Create New Project' button.,Leave the project name field empty.,Fill in optional fields if any.,Click 'Save' or 'Create Project'.
expected_result: System displays a validation error indicating that the project name is required. The project is not created.

title: Create a project with project name at maximum character boundary
preconditions: User is logged in with project management permissions. The maximum allowed project name length is known (e.g., 255 characters).
steps: Navigate to the Project Management section.,Click on 'Create New Project' button.,Enter a project name exactly at the maximum allowed character limit (e.g., 255 characters).,Fill in all other required fields with valid data.,Click 'Save' or 'Create Project'.
expected_result: Project is created successfully with the maximum-length project name stored and displayed correctly without truncation or error.

title: Attempt to create a project with a name exceeding maximum character limit
preconditions: User is logged in with project management permissions. The maximum allowed project name length is known (e.g., 255 characters).
steps: Navigate to the Project Management section.,Click on 'Create New Project' button.,Enter a project name that exceeds the maximum allowed character limit (e.g., 256 characters).,Fill in all other required fields with valid data.,Click 'Save' or 'Create Project'.
expected_result: System displays a validation error indicating the project name exceeds the maximum allowed length. The project is not created.

title: Successfully create a new project with all fields including optional end date
preconditions: User is logged in as a Project Manager. The project creation form is accessible.
steps: Navigate to the 'Create New Project' page,Enter a valid project name (e.g., 'Alpha Project'),Enter a valid project description (e.g., 'This is a test project'),Enter a valid start date (e.g., '2024-01-15'),Enter a valid end date (e.g., '2024-12-31'),Click the 'Create Project' button
expected_result: The project is created successfully with all provided fields. A success message is displayed and the new project appears in the project list with the correct name, description, start date, and end date.

title: Successfully create a new project without the optional end date
preconditions: User is logged in as a Project Manager. The project creation form is accessible.
steps: Navigate to the 'Create New Project' page,Enter a valid project name (e.g., 'Beta Project'),Enter a valid project description (e.g., 'Project without end date'),Enter a valid start date (e.g., '2024-03-01'),Leave the end date field empty,Click the 'Create Project' button
expected_result: The project is created successfully without an end date. A success message is displayed and the project appears in the project list with the end date shown as empty or 'N/A'.

title: Attempt to create a project with missing required fields (name, description, start date)
preconditions: User is logged in as a Project Manager. The project creation form is accessible.
steps: Navigate to the 'Create New Project' page,Leave the project name field empty,Leave the description field empty,Leave the start date field empty,Click the 'Create Project' button
expected_result: The system displays validation error messages indicating that project name, description, and start date are required fields. The project is NOT created.

title: Attempt to create a project where end date is before start date
preconditions: User is logged in as a Project Manager. The project creation form is accessible.
steps: Navigate to the 'Create New Project' page,Enter a valid project name (e.g., 'Gamma Project'),Enter a valid project description,Enter a start date of '2024-06-01',Enter an end date of '2024-01-01' (before the start date),Click the 'Create Project' button
expected_result: The system displays a validation error message indicating that the end date must be on or after the start date. The project is NOT created.

title: Attempt to create a project with start date equal to end date (boundary condition)
preconditions: User is logged in as a Project Manager. The project creation form is accessible.
steps: Navigate to the 'Create New Project' page,Enter a valid project name (e.g., 'Delta Project'),Enter a valid project description,Enter a start date of '2024-09-15',Enter an end date of '2024-09-15' (same as start date),Click the 'Create Project' button
expected_result: The system accepts the same date for both start and end date and creates the project successfully, displaying a success message and showing the project in the project list with matching start and end dates.

title: Project Manager successfully adds a team member to a project
preconditions: A Project Manager is logged in. A valid project exists. A registered user (non-member) is available to be added.
steps: Log in as a Project Manager.,Navigate to the project management section.,Select an existing project.,Click on 'Add Team Member'.,Search for a registered user by name or email.,Select the user from the search results.,Confirm the addition by clicking 'Add'.
expected_result: The selected user is successfully added to the project team. The user appears in the project's team member list. A confirmation message is displayed.

title: Project Manager successfully removes a team member from a project
preconditions: A Project Manager is logged in. A valid project exists with at least two team members.
steps: Log in as a Project Manager.,Navigate to the project management section.,Select an existing project.,View the current team member list.,Click 'Remove' next to a team member.,Confirm the removal in the confirmation dialog.
expected_result: The team member is removed from the project. The member no longer appears in the project's team list. A confirmation message is displayed.

title: Non-Project Manager role attempts to add a team member
preconditions: A user with a non-Project Manager role (e.g., Developer or Viewer) is logged in. A valid project exists.
steps: Log in as a user with a Developer or Viewer role.,Navigate to the project management section.,Select an existing project.,Attempt to access or click the 'Add Team Member' option.
expected_result: The 'Add Team Member' option is either hidden or disabled. If accessed directly via URL, the system returns an authorization error (e.g., HTTP 403 Forbidden). No team member is added.

title: Project Manager attempts to add a non-existent or unregistered user
preconditions: A Project Manager is logged in. A valid project exists.
steps: Log in as a Project Manager.,Navigate to the project management section.,Select an existing project.,Click on 'Add Team Member'.,Search for a user with an email or name that does not exist in the system.,Attempt to confirm the addition.
expected_result: The system displays an appropriate error message such as 'User not found'. No unregistered user is added to the project. The team member list remains unchanged.

title: Project Manager attempts to remove the last remaining team member from a project
preconditions: A Project Manager is logged in. A valid project exists with exactly one team member remaining.
steps: Log in as a Project Manager.,Navigate to the project management section.,Select a project that has only one team member.,Click 'Remove' next to the sole remaining team member.,Confirm the removal in the confirmation dialog.
expected_result: The system either prevents the removal with a warning message (e.g., 'A project must have at least one team member') or allows it and reflects an empty team list. The behavior aligns with the defined business rules for minimum team size.

title: Successfully archive an active project
preconditions: User is logged in with sufficient permissions. At least one active project exists in the system.
steps: Navigate to the project list page.,Select an active project.,Click on the 'Archive' option for the selected project.,Confirm the archive action when prompted.
expected_result: The project is archived successfully. A success confirmation message is displayed. The project no longer appears in the default project list. All project data remains intact and accessible via archived projects view.

title: Archived project is hidden from default project list but data is preserved
preconditions: User is logged in. A project has been successfully archived.
steps: Navigate to the default project list page.,Search or scroll through the default project list for the archived project.,Navigate to the archived projects section or apply the 'Archived' filter.,Select the archived project and verify its data (name, description, tasks, members, etc.).
expected_result: The archived project does not appear in the default project list. When viewing the archived projects section, the project is listed. All original project data (name, description, tasks, members, history) is fully preserved and accessible.

title: Attempt to archive a project without sufficient permissions
preconditions: User is logged in with a role that does not have permission to archive projects (e.g., read-only or basic member). At least one active project exists.
steps: Navigate to the project list page.,Select an active project.,Attempt to find and click the 'Archive' option for the selected project.
expected_result: The 'Archive' option is either not visible or is disabled for the user. If the action is attempted via direct API call, the system returns a 403 Forbidden error. The project remains active and visible in the default project list.

title: Attempt to archive an already archived project
preconditions: User is logged in with archive permissions. At least one project is already in the archived state.
steps: Navigate to the archived projects section.,Select an already archived project.,Attempt to click the 'Archive' option again on the archived project.
expected_result: The system either does not display an 'Archive' option for an already archived project, or displays an appropriate error/info message such as 'Project is already archived.' No duplicate archiving or data corruption occurs.

title: Archive the last remaining active project in the system
preconditions: User is logged in with archive permissions. Exactly one active project exists in the system and all other projects are already archived.
steps: Navigate to the default project list page and confirm only one active project is visible.,Select the last active project.,Click the 'Archive' option for the project.,Confirm the archive action when prompted.,Return to the default project list page.
expected_result: The last active project is archived successfully. The default project list is now empty (or displays a message such as 'No active projects found'). The archived project's data remains fully intact and is accessible through the archived projects view.

title: Valid date range: end date after start date
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to create or edit a project. A project form is open.
steps: Navigate to the project creation or edit page.,Enter a start date of '2024-01-01'.,Enter an end date of '2024-12-31'.,Submit or save the project.
expected_result: The project is saved successfully with the start date '2024-01-01' and end date '2024-12-31'. No error message is displayed.

title: Invalid date range: end date earlier than start date
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to create or edit a project. A project form is open.
steps: Navigate to the project creation or edit page.,Enter a start date of '2024-06-15'.,Enter an end date of '2024-03-01' (before the start date).,Attempt to submit or save the project.
expected_result: The system prevents saving and displays a validation error message such as 'End date cannot be earlier than start date.'

title: Boundary: end date equals start date
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to create or edit a project. A project form is open.
steps: Navigate to the project creation or edit page.,Enter a start date of '2024-05-10'.,Enter an end date of '2024-05-10' (same as start date).,Submit or save the project.
expected_result: The project is saved successfully. The system accepts an end date equal to the start date (same-day project), and no error message is displayed.

title: Boundary: end date one day before start date
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to create or edit a project. A project form is open.
steps: Navigate to the project creation or edit page.,Enter a start date of '2024-05-10'.,Enter an end date of '2024-05-09' (one day before start date).,Attempt to submit or save the project.
expected_result: The system prevents saving and displays a validation error message indicating that the end date must not be earlier than the start date.

title: Editing existing project: update end date to be earlier than start date
preconditions: User is logged in. An existing project exists with start date '2024-03-01' and end date '2024-09-30'.
steps: Navigate to the edit page of the existing project.,Change the end date to '2024-02-01' (before the existing start date).,Attempt to save the changes.
expected_result: The system rejects the update and displays an error message preventing the end date from being set earlier than the start date. The project retains its original end date.

title: Successfully duplicate an existing project with task list
preconditions: User is logged in as a Project Manager. At least one existing project exists with tasks, comments, and attachments.
steps: Navigate to the Projects list page.,Select an existing project that has tasks, comments, and attachments.,Click the 'Duplicate Project' button or option.,Confirm the duplication action when prompted.,Verify the duplicated project appears in the Projects list.,Open the duplicated project and inspect its contents.
expected_result: A new project is created as a copy of the original. The duplicated project contains all tasks from the original project. Comments and attachments are NOT present in the duplicated project. The duplicated project has a distinct name (e.g., 'Copy of [Original Project Name]').

title: Verify non-Project Manager role cannot duplicate a project
preconditions: User is logged in with a role other than Project Manager (e.g., Developer or Viewer). At least one existing project is available.
steps: Navigate to the Projects list page.,Select an existing project.,Attempt to find or click the 'Duplicate Project' button or option.,Observe the system response.
expected_result: The 'Duplicate Project' option is either not visible or is disabled for non-Project Manager roles. If the action is attempted via direct URL or API, the system returns an authorization error (e.g., HTTP 403 Forbidden) and no duplicate project is created.

title: Duplicate a project that has zero tasks
preconditions: User is logged in as a Project Manager. An existing project with no tasks, but with comments and attachments, is available.
steps: Navigate to the Projects list page.,Select the existing project that has no tasks.,Click the 'Duplicate Project' button or option.,Confirm the duplication action.,Open the duplicated project and inspect its contents.
expected_result: A new project is successfully created as a duplicate. The duplicated project has an empty task list (mirroring the original). No comments or attachments are present in the duplicated project.

title: Verify comments and attachments are strictly excluded from duplicated project
preconditions: User is logged in as a Project Manager. An existing project has at least 3 tasks, each with multiple comments and file attachments.
steps: Navigate to the Projects list page.,Select the target project with tasks, comments, and attachments.,Click the 'Duplicate Project' button.,Confirm the duplication action.,Open the duplicated project.,Navigate to each duplicated task and check for comments.,Navigate to each duplicated task and check for attachments.,Also check the project-level comments and attachments.
expected_result: All tasks from the original project are present in the duplicated project. Zero comments exist at both the project level and task level in the duplicated project. Zero attachments exist at both the project level and task level in the duplicated project.

title: Duplicate a project with maximum number of tasks
preconditions: User is logged in as a Project Manager. An existing project contains the maximum allowed number of tasks (e.g., system-defined limit such as 1000 tasks), each with comments and attachments.
steps: Navigate to the Projects list page.,Select the project containing the maximum number of tasks.,Click the 'Duplicate Project' button.,Confirm the duplication action.,Wait for the system to complete the duplication process.,Open the duplicated project and verify the task count.,Verify no comments or attachments are present.
expected_result: The system successfully duplicates the project without errors or timeouts. The duplicated project contains exactly the same number of tasks as the original (up to the maximum limit). No comments or attachments are copied. The system displays a success message upon completion.

title: Create a new task successfully
preconditions: User is authenticated and has access to the task management module.
steps: Log in to the application with valid credentials.,Navigate to the Task Management section.,Click on 'Create New Task'.,Fill in all required fields: Title, Description, Due Date, and Priority.,Click 'Save' or 'Submit'.
expected_result: The new task is created and displayed in the task list with all entered details correctly saved.

title: Edit an existing task successfully
preconditions: User is authenticated and at least one task exists in the system.
steps: Log in to the application with valid credentials.,Navigate to the Task Management section.,Select an existing task from the task list.,Click 'Edit'.,Update the task title and due date.,Click 'Save'.
expected_result: The task is updated with the new values and the changes are reflected immediately in the task list.

title: Create a task with missing required fields
preconditions: User is authenticated and is on the Create New Task page.
steps: Log in to the application with valid credentials.,Navigate to the Task Management section.,Click on 'Create New Task'.,Leave the 'Title' field empty.,Fill in the remaining optional fields.,Click 'Save' or 'Submit'.
expected_result: The system displays a validation error message indicating that the Title field is required and the task is not created.

title: Create a task with maximum allowed character length in Title field
preconditions: User is authenticated and is on the Create New Task page. Maximum character limit for Task Title is known (e.g., 255 characters).
steps: Log in to the application with valid credentials.,Navigate to the Task Management section.,Click on 'Create New Task'.,Enter exactly 255 characters in the 'Title' field.,Fill in all other required fields with valid data.,Click 'Save'.
expected_result: The task is created successfully with the 255-character title stored and displayed correctly without truncation or error.

title: Create a task with Title exceeding maximum character limit
preconditions: User is authenticated and is on the Create New Task page. Maximum character limit for Task Title is known (e.g., 255 characters).
steps: Log in to the application with valid credentials.,Navigate to the Task Management section.,Click on 'Create New Task'.,Enter 256 or more characters in the 'Title' field.,Fill in all other required fields with valid data.,Click 'Save'.
expected_result: The system displays a validation error message indicating the title exceeds the maximum allowed length and the task is not created.

title: Successfully create a task with all required fields
preconditions: User is logged in as a Contributor and has access to an existing project.
steps: Navigate to the project dashboard.,Click on 'Create Task' button.,Enter a valid title (e.g., 'Design Homepage').,Enter a valid description (e.g., 'Create wireframes for the homepage layout').,Set a due date to a future date (e.g., 2025-12-31).,Select a valid assignee from the dropdown list.,Click 'Save' or 'Submit'.
expected_result: The task is created successfully and appears in the project task list with the correct title, description, due date, and assignee displayed.

title: Attempt to create a task with missing required fields
preconditions: User is logged in as a Contributor and has access to an existing project.
steps: Navigate to the project dashboard.,Click on 'Create Task' button.,Leave the title field empty.,Leave the description field empty.,Do not set a due date.,Do not select an assignee.,Click 'Save' or 'Submit'.
expected_result: The system displays validation error messages indicating that all required fields (title, description, due date, assignee) must be filled in. The task is NOT created.

title: Create a task with a due date set to today's date (boundary: current date)
preconditions: User is logged in as a Contributor and has access to an existing project.
steps: Navigate to the project dashboard.,Click on 'Create Task' button.,Enter a valid title (e.g., 'Urgent Fix').,Enter a valid description.,Set the due date to today's current date.,Select a valid assignee.,Click 'Save' or 'Submit'.
expected_result: The task is created successfully with today's date as the due date and all fields are saved correctly.

title: Attempt to create a task with a due date in the past
preconditions: User is logged in as a Contributor and has access to an existing project.
steps: Navigate to the project dashboard.,Click on 'Create Task' button.,Enter a valid title.,Enter a valid description.,Set the due date to a past date (e.g., 2020-01-01).,Select a valid assignee.,Click 'Save' or 'Submit'.
expected_result: The system displays a validation error indicating that the due date cannot be in the past. The task is NOT created.

title: Verify that a non-Contributor role cannot create a task
preconditions: User is logged in with a role that is NOT a Contributor (e.g., Viewer/Guest) and has access to an existing project.
steps: Navigate to the project dashboard.,Attempt to locate or click the 'Create Task' button.,If accessible, fill in all required fields: title, description, due date, and assignee.,Click 'Save' or 'Submit'.
expected_result: The 'Create Task' option is either not visible or disabled for non-Contributor roles. If the action is attempted, the system returns an authorization error and the task is NOT created.

title: Assign a task to exactly one user successfully
preconditions: At least one task exists in the system in an unassigned state. At least two registered users (User A and User B) exist.
steps: Log in as an admin or authorized user.,Navigate to the task management section.,Select an unassigned task.,Assign the task to User A.,Save the assignment.,Verify the task details show User A as the sole assignee.
expected_result: The task is successfully assigned to User A. Only User A is listed as the assignee. No other users are associated with the task.

title: Reassign a task from one user to another user
preconditions: A task exists that is already assigned to User A. User B is a registered user in the system.
steps: Log in as an admin or authorized user.,Navigate to the task management section.,Select the task currently assigned to User A.,Reassign the task to User B.,Save the updated assignment.,Verify the task details show only User B as the assignee.,Verify User A is no longer listed as an assignee for this task.
expected_result: The task is reassigned to User B only. User A is removed as assignee. The task has exactly one assignee at all times during and after the reassignment.

title: Attempt to assign a task to multiple users simultaneously
preconditions: A task exists in the system. At least two registered users (User A and User B) exist. The UI or API allows selection of multiple users.
steps: Log in as an admin or authorized user.,Navigate to the task management section.,Select an existing task.,Attempt to assign the task to both User A and User B at the same time (e.g., via multi-select in UI or by sending both user IDs in an API request).,Submit the assignment request.,Observe the system response.
expected_result: The system rejects the multi-user assignment. An appropriate error message is displayed (e.g., 'A task can only be assigned to one user at a time.'). The task retains its previous assignment state unchanged.

title: Attempt to assign a task without selecting any user
preconditions: A task exists in the system in an unassigned or assigned state.
steps: Log in as an admin or authorized user.,Navigate to the task management section.,Select an existing task.,Open the assignment dialog or form.,Leave the user field blank or deselect any currently selected user.,Attempt to save the assignment with no user selected.,Observe the system response.
expected_result: The system either prevents saving with a validation error (e.g., 'Please select a user to assign this task.') or successfully unassigns the task, depending on business rules. The task is never assigned to more than one user.

title: Verify task assignment persists correctly when the assigned user is deleted
preconditions: A task is currently assigned to User A. An admin account is available.
steps: Log in as an admin.,Delete or deactivate User A from the system.,Navigate to the task that was assigned to User A.,Check the assignee field of the task.,Attempt to view or edit the task assignment.
expected_result: The system handles the deletion gracefully. The task either becomes unassigned or displays a placeholder indicating the original assignee no longer exists. The task does not become assigned to multiple users or enter an invalid state.

title: Verify task can be set to 'To Do' status
preconditions: User is logged in and has permission to create/edit tasks. At least one task exists in the system.
steps: Navigate to the task management section,Create a new task or open an existing task,Set the task status to 'To Do',Save the changes,Verify the task status is displayed as 'To Do'
expected_result: The task status is successfully set to 'To Do' and displayed correctly in the UI and any status listings.

title: Verify task status transitions through all valid statuses: To Do → In Progress → In Review → Done
preconditions: User is logged in with task edit permissions. A task exists with status 'To Do'.
steps: Open a task currently in 'To Do' status,Change the task status to 'In Progress' and save,Verify the status displays as 'In Progress',Change the task status to 'In Review' and save,Verify the status displays as 'In Review',Change the task status to 'Done' and save,Verify the status displays as 'Done'
expected_result: The task successfully transitions through all four statuses (To Do, In Progress, In Review, Done), each being saved and displayed correctly at every step.

title: Verify that an invalid/unsupported task status cannot be assigned
preconditions: User is logged in with task edit permissions. A task exists in the system.
steps: Open an existing task,Attempt to set the task status to an unsupported value (e.g., 'Cancelled', 'Pending', or an empty string) via the UI or API,Attempt to save the change
expected_result: The system rejects the invalid status value and displays an appropriate error message. The task retains its previous valid status. Only 'To Do', 'In Progress', 'In Review', and 'Done' are available options.

title: Verify all four task statuses appear as selectable options in the status dropdown
preconditions: User is logged in with task edit permissions. A task is open for editing.
steps: Open a task for editing,Click or interact with the status selection field/dropdown,Observe all available status options listed
expected_result: Exactly four status options are available: 'To Do', 'In Progress', 'In Review', and 'Done'. No additional or missing statuses are present.

title: Verify task status is persisted correctly after page refresh or re-login
preconditions: User is logged in. A task exists and its status has been recently updated (e.g., set to 'In Review').
steps: Open a task and change its status to 'In Review',Save the task,Refresh the browser page,Re-open the task and verify the status,Log out and log back in,Re-open the same task and verify the status again
expected_result: The task status 'In Review' is correctly persisted and displayed after both page refresh and re-login, confirming data is saved to the backend.

title: Create a subtask under a parent task (Level 1 nesting)
preconditions: User is logged in. A parent task exists in the system.
steps: Navigate to the parent task.,Select the option to add a subtask.,Enter valid details for the subtask.,Save the subtask.
expected_result: The subtask is successfully created under the parent task at nesting level 1 and is visible in the task hierarchy.

title: Create a subtask under a Level 1 subtask (Level 2 nesting)
preconditions: User is logged in. A parent task with a Level 1 subtask already exists in the system.
steps: Navigate to the Level 1 subtask.,Select the option to add a subtask.,Enter valid details for the new subtask.,Save the subtask.
expected_result: The subtask is successfully created at nesting level 2 under the Level 1 subtask and is visible in the task hierarchy.

title: Attempt to create a subtask beyond maximum nesting depth (Level 3)
preconditions: User is logged in. A task hierarchy exists with a parent task, a Level 1 subtask, and a Level 2 subtask.
steps: Navigate to the Level 2 subtask.,Attempt to select the option to add a subtask.,If the option is available, enter valid details and attempt to save.
expected_result: The system prevents creation of a subtask at Level 3. Either the 'Add Subtask' option is disabled/hidden, or an error message is displayed stating the maximum nesting depth of two levels has been reached.

title: Verify maximum nesting depth boundary at exactly Level 2
preconditions: User is logged in. A parent task and a Level 1 subtask exist in the system.
steps: Navigate to the Level 1 subtask.,Add a subtask to it to reach Level 2.,Save the Level 2 subtask.,Verify the Level 2 subtask is saved successfully.,Attempt to add a further subtask to the Level 2 subtask.
expected_result: The Level 2 subtask is saved successfully (boundary accepted). Attempting to add a Level 3 subtask is rejected by the system, confirming Level 2 is the maximum allowed depth.

title: Verify task hierarchy display reflects correct nesting levels
preconditions: User is logged in. A parent task with a Level 1 subtask and a Level 2 subtask has been created.
steps: Navigate to the task list or project board.,Locate the parent task.,Expand the parent task to view its subtasks.,Expand the Level 1 subtask to view its subtasks.
expected_result: The system correctly displays the full hierarchy: Parent Task → Level 1 Subtask → Level 2 Subtask, with appropriate visual indentation or hierarchy indicators for each level.

title: Notification sent to assignee when a new task is assigned
preconditions: User account exists, email notifications are enabled, and the task management system is operational.
steps: Log in as a manager or admin user.,Create a new task.,Assign the task to an existing user (assignee).,Save/submit the task assignment.,Check the assignee's notification inbox (email or in-app notification).
expected_result: The assignee receives a notification informing them that a new task has been assigned to them, including relevant task details such as task name and due date.

title: Notification sent to new assignee when a task is reassigned
preconditions: User accounts exist for both original and new assignees, a task is already assigned to the original assignee, and email notifications are enabled.
steps: Log in as a manager or admin user.,Navigate to an existing task that is assigned to User A.,Reassign the task from User A to User B.,Save/submit the reassignment.,Check User B's notification inbox (email or in-app notification).,Check User A's notification inbox to confirm no unexpected notifications.
expected_result: User B (new assignee) receives a notification that the task has been assigned to them. The notification contains relevant task details. User A does not receive a new assignment notification.

title: No notification sent when task assignment remains unchanged
preconditions: A task is already assigned to a user, and email notifications are enabled.
steps: Log in as a manager or admin user.,Navigate to an existing task assigned to User A.,Edit the task details (e.g., description or due date) without changing the assignee.,Save the changes.,Check User A's notification inbox.
expected_result: User A does not receive a task assignment notification since the assignee was not changed.

title: Notification behavior when assignee has no valid email address
preconditions: A user account exists with no valid email address configured, and email is the only notification channel.
steps: Log in as a manager or admin user.,Create or locate a task.,Assign the task to a user whose email address is missing or invalid.,Save/submit the task assignment.,Check system logs or error/notification queue for failures.
expected_result: The system handles the invalid/missing email gracefully (e.g., logs an error or shows an alert), does not crash, and ideally notifies an administrator of the delivery failure.

title: Notification sent when a task is simultaneously reassigned to multiple users (boundary: bulk reassignment)
preconditions: Multiple user accounts exist, notifications are enabled, and the system supports bulk task reassignment.
steps: Log in as a manager or admin user.,Select the maximum number of tasks supported by the system for bulk reassignment.,Reassign all selected tasks to a new assignee.,Submit the bulk reassignment.,Check the new assignee's notification inbox.
