expected_result: The new assignee receives a notification for each reassigned task (or a consolidated notification for bulk reassignment), and no notifications are dropped or duplicated beyond the expected count.

title: Successfully attach a file to a collaboration item
preconditions: User is logged in. A collaboration item (e.g., task or project) exists. User has permission to add attachments.
steps: Navigate to the collaboration item detail page.,Click the 'Attach File' button.,Select a valid file (e.g., PDF, 2 MB) from the local file system.,Click 'Upload' or 'Confirm'.,Observe the attachment list on the collaboration item.
expected_result: The file is successfully uploaded and appears in the attachment list with the correct file name, size, and upload timestamp.

title: Invite a collaborator and verify they receive access
preconditions: User is logged in as an owner or admin of a project. The invitee has a registered account in the system.
steps: Navigate to the collaboration item or project settings.,Click 'Invite Collaborator'.,Enter the valid email address of the invitee.,Select an appropriate role (e.g., Editor).,Click 'Send Invitation'.,Log in as the invitee and accept the invitation.,Verify the invitee can view and interact with the collaboration item.
expected_result: The invitee successfully gains access to the collaboration item with the assigned role and can perform actions permitted by that role.

title: Attempt to upload a file exceeding the maximum allowed size
preconditions: User is logged in. A collaboration item exists. The system enforces a maximum attachment size (e.g., 25 MB).
steps: Navigate to the collaboration item detail page.,Click the 'Attach File' button.,Select a file that exceeds the maximum allowed size (e.g., 26 MB).,Click 'Upload' or 'Confirm'.,Observe the system response.
expected_result: The system rejects the upload and displays a clear error message indicating the file exceeds the maximum allowed size. No file is added to the attachment list.

title: Upload a file exactly at the maximum allowed size boundary
preconditions: User is logged in. A collaboration item exists. The system enforces a maximum attachment size (e.g., 25 MB). A test file of exactly 25 MB is available.
steps: Navigate to the collaboration item detail page.,Click the 'Attach File' button.,Select a file that is exactly 25 MB.,Click 'Upload' or 'Confirm'.,Observe the system response.
expected_result: The file at the exact boundary size is accepted and successfully uploaded. It appears in the attachment list with the correct metadata.

title: Unauthorized user attempts to access or download an attachment
preconditions: A collaboration item exists with at least one attachment. A second user exists who has NOT been granted access to this collaboration item.
steps: Log in as the unauthorized user.,Attempt to directly access the attachment URL (e.g., via a direct link or API call).,Observe the system response.
expected_result: The system denies access and returns an appropriate authorization error (e.g., HTTP 403 Forbidden). The unauthorized user cannot view or download the attachment.

title: Add a plain text comment to a task
preconditions: User is logged in, has access to a project, and a task exists within the project.
steps: Navigate to the project and open an existing task.,Locate the comments section on the task detail page.,Type a plain text comment (e.g., 'This task is in progress.') in the comment input field.,Click the 'Submit' or 'Add Comment' button.
expected_result: The plain text comment is saved and displayed in the comments section of the task with the correct author name and timestamp.

title: Add a comment with a valid @mention of a project member
preconditions: User is logged in, has access to a project with at least two members, and a task exists within the project.
steps: Navigate to the project and open an existing task.,Locate the comments section on the task detail page.,Type '@' followed by the username of another project member (e.g., '@john_doe This needs your review.').,Verify that an autocomplete suggestion appears for the mentioned member.,Select the suggested member from the autocomplete list.,Click the 'Submit' or 'Add Comment' button.
expected_result: The comment is saved and displayed with the @mention highlighted or formatted. The mentioned user receives a notification.

title: Add a comment with @mention of a user not in the project
preconditions: User is logged in, has access to a project, a task exists, and a second user exists in the system but is NOT a member of the current project.
steps: Navigate to the project and open an existing task.,Locate the comments section on the task detail page.,Type '@' followed by the username of a user who is not a project member (e.g., '@external_user').,Observe the autocomplete suggestions.,Attempt to submit the comment with the non-member @mention.
expected_result: The non-member username does not appear in the autocomplete suggestions. If submitted manually, the @mention is not resolved/highlighted and no notification is sent to the non-member user.

title: Attempt to submit an empty comment
preconditions: User is logged in and has a task open with the comments section visible.
steps: Navigate to the project and open an existing task.,Locate the comments section on the task detail page.,Leave the comment input field empty.,Click the 'Submit' or 'Add Comment' button.
expected_result: The system prevents submission and displays an appropriate validation error message (e.g., 'Comment cannot be empty.'). No empty comment is saved.

title: Add a comment at the maximum allowed character boundary
preconditions: User is logged in, has access to a project, a task exists, and the maximum character limit for comments is known (e.g., 5000 characters).
steps: Navigate to the project and open an existing task.,Locate the comments section on the task detail page.,Enter a plain text comment that is exactly at the maximum allowed character limit (e.g., 5000 characters).,Click the 'Submit' or 'Add Comment' button.,Then attempt to enter a comment that exceeds the maximum character limit by one character (e.g., 5001 characters) and click 'Submit'.
expected_result: The comment at exactly the maximum character limit is saved and displayed successfully. The comment exceeding the limit is rejected with an appropriate error message (e.g., 'Comment exceeds maximum allowed length.').

title: Valid @mention triggers notification to mentioned user
preconditions: Two registered users exist: User A (commenter) and User B (mentioned). User B has a valid email/notification preference enabled. User A is logged in and viewing a post/item with a comment section.
steps: Log in as User A,Navigate to a post or item that has a comment section,Create a new comment containing '@UserB followed by some text',Submit the comment,Log in as User B or check User B's notification inbox
expected_result: User B receives a notification (in-app and/or email) indicating they were @mentioned by User A in the comment. The notification includes relevant context such as a link to the comment.

title: Multiple @mentions in a single comment notify all mentioned users
preconditions: Three registered users exist: User A (commenter), User B, and User C. All users have notifications enabled. User A is logged in.
steps: Log in as User A,Navigate to a post or item with a comment section,Create a new comment containing '@UserB and @UserC please review this',Submit the comment,Check notification inboxes for both User B and User C
expected_result: Both User B and User C each receive a separate notification informing them they were @mentioned in the comment by User A.

title: Non-existent username @mention does not trigger notification
preconditions: User A is logged in. The username '@NonExistentUser999' does not exist in the system.
steps: Log in as User A,Navigate to a post or item with a comment section,Create a new comment containing '@NonExistentUser999 check this out',Submit the comment,Verify no notification is sent to any user for this mention
expected_result: The comment is submitted successfully, but no notification is generated for the non-existent username. The system may optionally display a warning that the mentioned user was not found.

title: User @mentioning themselves does not trigger a self-notification
preconditions: User A is logged in with notifications enabled.
steps: Log in as User A,Navigate to a post or item with a comment section,Create a new comment containing '@UserA this is a self-mention',Submit the comment,Check User A's notification inbox
expected_result: The comment is submitted successfully. User A does not receive a notification for mentioning themselves, preventing unnecessary self-notification noise.

title: Boundary: @mention at the very beginning and very end of a comment
preconditions: User A (commenter) and User B are registered and have notifications enabled. User A is logged in.
steps: Log in as User A,Navigate to a post or item with a comment section,Create a comment where '@UserB' appears at the very first character of the comment (e.g., '@UserB please see this'),Submit the comment and verify User B receives a notification,Create another comment where '@UserB' appears at the very last position (e.g., 'Please review this @UserB'),Submit the comment and verify User B receives a notification
expected_result: In both boundary cases, User B receives a notification for being @mentioned, confirming the mention detection works regardless of the position of the @mention within the comment text.

title: Successfully attach a valid file under 25 MB to a task
preconditions: User is logged in, a task exists, and a valid file (e.g., PDF, 10 MB) is available on the local machine.
steps: Navigate to the task detail page.,Click the 'Attach File' button.,Select a file that is 10 MB in size.,Click 'Upload' or 'Attach'.,Verify the attachment appears in the task's attachment list.
expected_result: The file is successfully attached to the task and is visible in the attachment list with correct file name and size displayed.

title: Reject file attachment exceeding 25 MB limit
preconditions: User is logged in, a task exists, and a file larger than 25 MB (e.g., 30 MB) is available on the local machine.
steps: Navigate to the task detail page.,Click the 'Attach File' button.,Select a file that is 30 MB in size.,Attempt to upload/attach the file.,Observe the system response.
expected_result: The system rejects the file upload and displays an appropriate error message such as 'File size exceeds the maximum limit of 25 MB.' The task remains unchanged with no file attached.

title: Attach a file exactly at the 25 MB boundary
preconditions: User is logged in, a task exists, and a file of exactly 25 MB is available on the local machine.
steps: Navigate to the task detail page.,Click the 'Attach File' button.,Select a file that is exactly 25 MB in size.,Click 'Upload' or 'Attach'.,Verify the system response.
expected_result: The file of exactly 25 MB is successfully attached to the task and is visible in the attachment list, confirming the boundary value is accepted.

title: Attempt to attach a file just above the 25 MB boundary (25 MB + 1 byte)
preconditions: User is logged in, a task exists, and a file of size 25 MB + 1 byte (26,214,401 bytes) is available on the local machine.
steps: Navigate to the task detail page.,Click the 'Attach File' button.,Select a file that is 25 MB + 1 byte in size.,Attempt to upload/attach the file.,Observe the system response.
expected_result: The system rejects the file and displays an error message indicating the file exceeds the 25 MB maximum size. No file is attached to the task.

title: Attempt to attach a file with no content (0 bytes / empty file)
preconditions: User is logged in, a task exists, and an empty file (0 bytes) is available on the local machine.
steps: Navigate to the task detail page.,Click the 'Attach File' button.,Select an empty file (0 bytes) from the local machine.,Attempt to upload/attach the file.,Observe the system response.
expected_result: The system either rejects the empty file with an appropriate error message (e.g., 'Cannot attach an empty file.') or handles it gracefully, ensuring no corrupted or zero-byte attachment is stored on the task.

title: Upload valid PDF attachment
preconditions: User is logged into the system and is on a page that supports file attachments.
steps: Navigate to the attachment upload section.,Click the 'Attach File' button.,Select a valid PDF file (e.g., document.pdf) from the local file system.,Click 'Upload' or 'Submit'.
expected_result: The PDF file is successfully uploaded and appears in the attachments list without any error messages.

title: Upload all supported file types (DOCX, XLSX, PNG, JPG, CSV)
preconditions: User is logged into the system and is on a page that supports file attachments. Sample files of each type are available locally.
steps: Navigate to the attachment upload section.,Click the 'Attach File' button and select a valid DOCX file; verify upload success.,Click the 'Attach File' button and select a valid XLSX file; verify upload success.,Click the 'Attach File' button and select a valid PNG file; verify upload success.,Click the 'Attach File' button and select a valid JPG file; verify upload success.,Click the 'Attach File' button and select a valid CSV file; verify upload success.
expected_result: Each of the supported file types (DOCX, XLSX, PNG, JPG, CSV) is successfully uploaded and listed in the attachments section without errors.

title: Upload unsupported file type is rejected
preconditions: User is logged into the system and is on a page that supports file attachments. An unsupported file (e.g., file.exe, file.txt, file.mp4) is available locally.
steps: Navigate to the attachment upload section.,Click the 'Attach File' button.,Select a file with an unsupported extension (e.g., malware.exe or document.txt).,Attempt to upload the file by clicking 'Upload' or 'Submit'.
expected_result: The system rejects the file and displays a clear error message indicating that the file type is not supported. The file does not appear in the attachments list.

title: Upload a file with a valid extension but mismatched MIME type (boundary/security)
preconditions: User is logged into the system. A file is prepared that has a supported extension (e.g., .pdf) but contains content of a different type (e.g., an executable renamed to document.pdf).
steps: Navigate to the attachment upload section.,Click the 'Attach File' button.,Select the disguised file (e.g., an executable renamed as document.pdf).,Attempt to upload the file.
expected_result: The system detects the MIME type mismatch and rejects the file, displaying an appropriate error message. The malicious file is not stored on the server.

title: Upload a file with zero bytes (empty file boundary case)
preconditions: User is logged into the system and is on a page that supports file attachments. A zero-byte file with a supported extension (e.g., empty.csv) is available locally.
steps: Navigate to the attachment upload section.,Click the 'Attach File' button.,Select the zero-byte file (e.g., empty.csv) from the local file system.,Attempt to upload the file by clicking 'Upload' or 'Submit'.
expected_result: The system either rejects the empty file with a meaningful validation message (e.g., 'File cannot be empty') or handles it gracefully without crashing or corrupting the system state.
